prep.summary: You and Praveena work closely on architecture strategy, team operations, and planning — recurring threads include the Products & Services catalog, horizontal planning prep, and team retention concerns. As of 5-19, the most active open items are Matt's departure and Vince's backfill, the Horizontal Planning meeting format shift, and key projects like TRAD Plus and DAMIT. Earlier meetings surfaced the AI requirements tool (built with Adam, received by Sarah Hudson but not yet industrialized) and Deepak's push for more tangible architecture deliverables.
tasks: Summarize the 5-13 meeting — cover Mark's health update, leadership transition anxiety, Rebecca's potential director move, and the AI requirements prototype status.
Summarize the 5-19 meeting — cover Matt's departure and the Vince backfill plan, the restructured Horizontal Planning format, and the key projects tracked (TRAD Plus, DAMIT, Telematics).

Note any unresolved threads across both meetings — specifically the AI tool industrialization question, Jeff's reassurance about architecture's value, and whether the Horizontal Planning meeting produced any outcomes worth flagging.
Be ready to discuss whether the Vince backfill has moved forward and if a formal transition plan for Matt was established.
Flag the Products & Services catalog from 5-6 as a potential standing item — confirm with Praveena whether SME feedback came in and if Deepak's concerns were mapped to the catalog.
prep.title: Sync with Praveena

prep.summary: The Q2 2026 Horizontal Planning meeting follows a format shift noted in the 5-19 Praveena sync — horizontal teams (Security, Infrastructure, Ops, Data, Contact Center, CFO) present their horizons and dependencies first, with architecture observing and engaging toward the end. Open threads feeding into this meeting include the Hi Marley/Claims 2-way SMS security escalation (unresolved risk acceptance dispute as of 5-18), Scan AI desktop deployment scope and governance (unresolved as of 5-19), and the TRAD Plus/Guidewire rebalance targeting 2031. The prep instructions call out four specific topic areas — data support and claims activity, D&S, future of underwriting, and Experience Intelligence E2E — plus new channel security support, all of which intersect active threads from recent meetings.
tasks: Review sent emails for any notes or summaries touching data support, current claims activity, D&S, future of underwriting, and Experience Intelligence E2E before the meeting starts.

Prepare a clear position on the Hi Marley security escalation — the business's risk-acceptance proposal was rejected by IT and Architecture as of 5-18, so know the current status and whether a decision has been forced ahead of this planning cycle.

Confirm Scan AI's deployment scope and funding status — the channel support security angle maps directly to the prep instruction asking for 'security for new channel support', and the 5-19 sync left desktop footprint and governance unresolved.

Identify which horizontal teams are presenting dependencies that touch underwriting modernization — specifically the TRAD Plus/Guidewire 2031 timeline and DAMIT pricing work flagged in the Praveena 5-19 notes — so you can engage precisely when architecture's window opens.
Note Vince's backfill of Matt's responsibilities as context for any resource or capacity questions that surface during the data or underwriting segments of the planning review.
prep.title: (Q2) 2026 Horizontal Planning

events.location: Eagle Hall A; | Zoom; EXEC CEN...

prep.summary: The P&C Sandbox/Lab Environment meetings have centered on standing up a dedicated test environment to support the 2026 rollout of Claims HQ, Text-to-Audio, and Visual Voicemail — with the Claims UX team (Rolando, Mary Lee, Slax) pushing for full admin rights, synthetic production data, and the ability to test at scale with real adjusters. From the 5/14 meeting, two threads remain open: Rolando was to send a formal requirements document to Mark for review, and the group needed to identify specific stakeholders from Dan Weaver's team and the architecture team to pull into planning. The concept of the 'P&C Lab' was still being socialized upward with leadership as of that last session.
tasks: Confirm whether Rolando sent the formal test environment requirements document — locate it or flag that it's still outstanding.
Come prepared with names from Dan Weaver's team and the architecture team to propose as stakeholders for the next planning phase.
Review the EMG Offsite notes for relevant infrastructure context — specifically the on-prem capacity discussion, which likely bears directly on where this sandbox environment gets hosted.
Clarify the current status of the 'P&C Lab' concept with leadership — determine whether socialization has moved forward or stalled since 5/14.

Be ready to discuss how the AI-assisted business analysis approach (used by Mark and Adam) could accelerate requirements definition for the sandbox if gaps in Business Architecture capability persist.
prep.title: P&C Sandbox/Lab Environment

events.summary: EMG connect on TWT Security issue

prep.summary: Rick is a recurring collaborator on the Two-Way Texting / Hi Marley implementation — the open thread running through every prior meeting is the security standoff between the new CISO Dan's hard-line MFA/PII stance and the business push to retire CCOM in favor of Hi Marley SMS. The most concrete unresolved items are: the NYDFS 2025 ruling pressure, the DLP and perimeter-security gaps that surfaced post-contract, and the timeline crunch (Claims HQ + 2-way texting targeting Oct/Nov 2026 but under pressure to compress to Q2). The prep instruction says to sync with Rick before the EMG connect — treat this meeting as your alignment checkpoint before that larger audience.
tasks: Confirm with Rick what specific security issue is being surfaced to EMG — clarify whether the focus is the NYDFS/MFA authentication gap, the DLP exposure, or both.
Align with Rick on the current status of the Hi Marley/CCOM transition — determine whether any decisions have been made since the 5/8 meeting about retiring CCOM or adjusting the security model.
Decide with Rick what position IT is presenting to EMG — establish whether the ask is a decision, an escalation, or an informational brief.
Review the Q2 vs. Oct/Nov timeline conflict — confirm whether the compressed delivery pressure from Gloria's miscommunication has changed the scope or resourcing picture before EMG hears it.
Prepare to address Dan's authentication requirements directly — know the current MFA gap status for inbound SMS data so you are not caught flat-footed in the EMG meeting.
prep.title: EMG connect on TWT Security issue
